Version History Spike — Canada Federal (Access to Information Act, A-1)
=======================================================================

Source: justicecanada/laws-lois-xml GitHub repository
Method: git history of eng/acts/A-1.xml across commits

The Justice Canada XML API (https://laws-lois.justice.gc.ca/eng/XML/A-1.xml)
does NOT support historical versions via ?pit= parameter. All requests return
the current consolidated text regardless of the pit value.

However, the official GitHub repository (justicecanada/laws-lois-xml) maintains
version history through git commits. Each "Laws Site Update" commit represents
a new consolidated snapshot.

Version 1: 2024-11-01 (Laws Site Update)
  Commit: d88024435e65b0f56dd545212826945be0b5babe
  File: eng/acts/A-1.xml
  Size: 485,914 bytes
  pit-date in XML: corresponds to consolidation as of 2024-10

Version 2: 2026-03-18 (Laws Site Update)
  Commit: 364726c551e1ecd1466511e8545a03759c0babd0
  File: eng/acts/A-1.xml
  Size: 485,447 bytes
  pit-date in XML: 2025-06-02

Full commit history for A-1.xml (10 versions observed via GitHub API):
  2026-03-18: Laws Site Update 2026-03-18
  2025-07-31: Laws Site Update 2025-07-31
  2025-06-24: Laws Site Update 2025-06-24
  2025-06-12: Laws Site Update 2025-06-12
  2025-04-17: Laws Site Update 2025-04-17
  2025-03-07: Laws Site Update 2025-03-07
  2025-02-06: Laws Site Update 2025-02-06
  2024-11-25: Laws Site Update 2024-11-25
  2024-11-13: Laws Site Update 2024-11-13
  2024-11-01: Laws Site Update 2024-11-01

Differences:
  Sizes differ: YES (485,914 vs 485,447 = 467 bytes difference)
  Stable identifier: A-1 (ConsolidatedNumber) across all versions
  Dates extractable: pit-date and lastAmendedDate in XML root attributes

Version history strategy:
  - Clone justicecanada/laws-lois-xml with full history
  - Walk git log per file to extract versions
  - Each commit's version of a file = one version of the law
  - Use commit date as effective_date for GIT_AUTHOR_DATE
  - Section-level lastAmendedDate and inforce-start-date provide
    finer granularity within each snapshot

Conclusion: GATE PASS
  - 2+ distinct versions extracted with dates from git history
  - Stable identifiers link versions (ConsolidatedNumber / InstrumentNumber)
  - Git-based version access is reliable and complete
  - No API rate limits for git clone/pull
